commit.message:
fix(model-3d): fix face splitting — snap line to plane + use triangle lookup

Three fixes for face splitting:
1. Snap line endpoints onto face plane before 2D projection (fixes
   floating-point errors from matrixWorld.invert() causing missed
   intersections)
2. Use triangleToHEFace for O(1) face lookup instead of findFaceAtPoint
   (avoids point-in-polygon edge cases at vertices/edges)
3. Include updatedAt in edge geometry key so edges re-render after splits

commit.message:
feat(model-3d): HEMesh as single source of truth — edge rendering + kernel extrude

Replace EdgesGeometry (which shows triangulation diagonals) with topology-aware
edge rendering from HEMesh. Add kernel extrudeFace operation for SketchUp-style
push-pull directly on the half-edge mesh, bypassing the Manifold backend.

- Add buildHEEdgeGeometry() to render only real topology edges
- Add extrudeFace() with 3 cases: face-on-solid, standalone, split face
- Wire push-pull to use kernel extrude when HEMesh is available
- Add heFaceId to FaceData for O(1) face lookup
- Update snap candidate generator to use HEMesh edges
- 62 kernel tests passing

commit.message:
fix(karabiner,tmux): bypass IME for tmux prefix key in terminal apps (#68)

## Background / 背景

日本語IMEがオンの状態でターミナル内の
`Ctrl+b`（tmuxプレフィックス）を押すと、IMEがキーを横取りしてtmuxに届かない問題があった。Claude
Code使用時に特に顕著で、`b1` などがそのまま入力されてしまっていた。

## Changes / 変更内容

- **Karabiner-Elements**: ターミナルアプリ（Ghostty, iTerm2, Terminal.app等）内で
`Ctrl+b` を押した際に、先に `英数` キーを送信してIMEをオフにしてから `Ctrl+b` を送信するルールを追加
- **tmux**: `escape-time 0`（エスケープシーケンス待機時間を0に短縮）、`focus-events
on`（フォーカスイベント転送）、`bind Space last-window` を追加
- **Claude settings**: `WebSearch` 許可を追加

## Impact scope / 影響範囲

- Karabiner-Elementsの入力処理（ターミナルアプリ内のみ）
- tmuxのキー入力応答性

## Testing / 動作確認

- [x] 日本語IMEオンの状態で `Ctrl+b` + ウィンドウ番号でtmuxウィンドウ切替が動作する
- [x] 英語入力モードでも従来通り動作する

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add anchor-based monotonicity correction (Strategy 6), coverage 93.7% → 94.7%

Phase 2 in fix_d_c_misread.py uses 57,745 C/D-free anchor addresses to build
a ground-truth frame→address trajectory. Frames with C/D-containing addresses
are checked against the interpolated expected address; if a C↔D swap brings
the address ≥$800 closer, the frame is relocated. This catches systematic
misread blocks (e.g. frames 14290-14340) where all neighbors share the wrong
address, defeating the Phase 1 ±10 neighbor heuristic.

Results: 7,514 frames relocated (388 Phase 1 + 7,325 Phase 2 - 199 deduped),
208 lines recovered in $0D050-$0DF70, 271 missing lines remaining.

commit.message:
fix: resolve bloqueadores de producao — schema corrompido e inadimplencia

Schema (build_schema.py + master_schema.json):
- Adiciona _VALID_FIELD_RE (^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]{0,63}$) no parse_excel
- Elimina 167 campos corrompidos (description rows do Excel tratados como field name)
- Schema rebuild: 2631 -> 2464 campos limpos

Inadimplencia (3 arquivos):
- semantic_dictionary: regras=['titulo_atraso'] (STATUS='A' AND VENCTO<CURRENT_DATE)
  em vez de titulo_aberto; adiciona aliases: inadimplente, vencidos, titulos vencidos
- ambiguity_analyzer: nao injeta em_aberto quando inadimplencia esta nas metricas
- sql_builder: skip time_refs quando inadimplencia in metrics (EMISSAO nao deve
  ser filtrado — temporal coberto por VENCTO < CURRENT_DATE)

SQL gerado correto:
  ticket_medio: SELECT AVG(VENDAS.TOTAL) ... GROUP BY CLIENTES.CODIGO, NOME, PAI, RAZAO
  inadimplencia: SELECT SUM(RECEBER.VRDUPL) WHERE STATUS='A' AND VENCTO < CURRENT_DATE

Add family spending tracker with invite codes, consent, and real-time feed

New "Spending Tracker" page in family mode replaces Bank Connect. Features:
- Parent generates a 6-digit invite code, shares with family members
- Members enter code in their BudgetWise app to request linking
- Parent approves/revokes each member individually
- Members toggle sharing on/off and choose: share all spending or
  only specific categories (e.g. hide personal purchases)
- When sharing is on, expenses auto-sync to the family feed
- Parent sees real-time spending feed grouped by member with totals
- Members can unlink at any time, deleting all shared data
- Includes SQL migration for Supabase tables with proper RLS policies

## Major Changes

### Hexo Upgrade

- Update Node.js version from 12 to 20 in GitHub Actions
- Add hexo-filter-mathjax for math formula support
- Configure hexo-renderer-marked with disableNunjucks for LaTeX compatibility

### NexT Theme Upgrade

- Migrate theme configuration to _config.next.yml

- Configure Disqus comments

### Removed Plugins
- hexo-generator-baidu-sitemap (no longer maintained)
- hexo-math (replaced by hexo-filter-mathjax)
- hexo-inject (functionality built into NexT 8.x)

- Add node_modules to .gitignore

  fit without scrolling

Phase 3: URL parsing, API model, and content decoding

- api.rs: LovableProjectRef validates scheme, domain, /projects/ prefix,
  and UUID; builds API endpoint URL. LovableClient wraps reqwest with
  minimal auth headers (authorization, accept, origin, referer).
- model.rs: SourceFiles/SourceFile serde models with sizeExceeded rename;
  validate() enforces contents presence invariant; get_data() decodes
  base64 binary or returns UTF-8 bytes.
- 13 new unit tests (7 URL parsing, 6 model decoding); 16 total passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Net-30 Credit Building add-on

Implements the Net-30 Credit Building à la carte add-on, purchasable
by any FYLR user regardless of their current plan tier.

Database
- Added `plan`, `application_fee_paid`, `application_fee_amount`, and
  `stripe_subscription_id` columns to the existing Net30Subscription
  model (migration 38c077f3e78f).

Pricing
- New app/services/pricing_config.py with:
    NET30_PLANS dict  (starter $24.99/mo · 1 tradeline $2K;
                       growth  $45.00/mo · 2 tradelines $2K+$5K)
    get_application_fee(user)  ($47.40 for Guided/Premium, $79.00 otherwise)
    get_stripe_price_id(plan)  (reads env vars for Stripe Price IDs)

Blueprint (app/net30.py — prefix /net30)
- GET  /net30/             landing page with plan cards + personalised fee
- POST /net30/apply        Stripe Checkout for one-time application fee
- GET  /net30/subscribe/<plan>  Stripe Checkout for monthly subscription
- GET  /net30/success      confirmation; marks subscription active
- GET  /net30/status       JSON status consumed by dashboard widget
- Registered in app/__init__.py with try/except ImportError guard.

Dashboard widget (templates/premium_dashboard.html)
- All users now see the Credit Building widget.
- Active subscriber: shows plan badge, tradelines, and next reporting date.
- No subscription: shows "Add Credit Building →" CTA to /net30/.

Templates (templates/net30/)
- index.html   Quiet Luxury landing page — Starter vs Growth plan cards,
               Metro 2 bureau chips, application fee callout, how-it-works,
               navbar/footer hidden during flow.
- success.html Post-payment confirmation with next-reporting date and
               next-steps checklist.

Tests: 75/75 endpoint tests pass (0 failures).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: connect graph runtime to codegen (CompiledNodeExecutor)

Graph nodes with `semantic: { formal: <expr> }` blocks now compile the
expression via lattice-codegen and execute it through the interpreter
when the graph runs. The node's input value is bound as the `input`
variable. This replaces the IdentityNode pass-through for nodes with
formal expressions, making `lattice run` actually compute.

- Add CompiledNodeExecutor in lattice-codegen/src/executor.rs
- Add Interpreter::set_variable() for pre-binding values
- Extract SemanticBlock.formal as NodeImpl::Expression in graph builder
- Wire compiled executors into scheduler in CLI cmd_run
- 8 new tests (unit + integration pipeline tests)

Closes #24

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: persist 3D GLB to Supabase Storage for permanent URLs

Meshy CDN URLs expire after ~3 days causing 502 errors. Now:
- Webhook uploads GLB to Supabase Storage on generation success
- GLB proxy also persists to Storage on first access / URL expiry
- creature-viewer loads Supabase URLs directly (no proxy needed)
- Supabase Storage bucket 'models-3d' created (public, 100MB limit)

Requires SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY env var for Storage uploads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Strip JS render sections to fix 30k input-token rate limit

After CSS stripping, the 74k-char <script> block still pushed total
input tokens to ~32-33k, exceeding the 30k/min limit on every call.

Add strip_js_render() which strips 5 rendering-only sections:
  • Component helpers (Pill, MetricCard, etc.) — 2,611 chars
  • DSEXChart return() JSX                     — 5,980 chars
  • TBillChart return() JSX                    — 4,859 chars
  • OilChart (keeps STATIC_DATA, strips rest)  — 7,310 chars
  • App() + ReactDOM mount                     — 7,493 chars
Total: 27,975 chars (~11k tokens) saved. Remaining HTML is ~18k tokens,
well under the 30k limit with headroom for prompt text and tool defs.

Each stripped section is replaced with a unique placeholder comment.
Claude is instructed to pass all placeholders through unchanged.
Originals are restored into Claude's output before writing files,
with anchor-based fallback if any placeholder is missing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove transparent-wipe strategy; force #111111 directly on section

Previous approach wiped section bg to transparent then tried to
re-apply dark — if the testid selector missed, white parent showed.
New approach: force #111111 directly on all divs/sections inside
stFileUploader. The dropzone is a <section> element (confirmed via
Streamlit 1.54.0 JS source). Also pins streamlit==1.54.0 to prevent
Cloud/local version mismatch causing DOM structure differences.
